Summary: Project Foundation
Description: Monorepo scaffolding, Docker, CI config, .gitignore, sidecar scaffold

Summary: Core Engine & Database
Description: Headless data layer with Drizzle ORM, PostgreSQL schema, repositories, services, hooks

Summary: REST API Server
Description: HTTP routes wrapping core services for frontend consumption

Summary: MCP Server
Description: stdio MCP server with tools, resources, and prompts for editor integration

Summary: File Bridge
Description: .decidr/ folder watcher for editor-agnostic file-based integration

Summary: Frontend — Shell & Board
Description: React app shell, kanban board, ticket cards, layout, stores

Summary: Frontend — Ticket Detail & Reasoning
Description: Detail modal, diff viewer, decision tree, step-by-step logs, comments, history

Summary: Frontend — Create & Panels
Description: Ticket creation modal, hooks log panel, stats dashboard

Summary: Tauri Desktop Shell
Description: Native desktop window, Node.js sidecar lifecycle, system tray

Summary: VS Code / Cursor Extension
Description: Sidebar panel, commands, API client for VS Code and Cursor

Summary: Interactive Documentation
Description: Doc site with live demos, API tester, schema viewer, tree demo

Summary: Editor Integration & ECC
Description: Config files, everything-claude-code integration, agent definitions

Summary: As a developer, I want a monorepo scaffold so that all packages share config and can be developed together
Acceptance Criteria: npm workspaces configured; tsconfig.base.json shared; npm install works at root; npm run dev starts server + web

Summary: As a developer, I want a local PostgreSQL instance so that I can develop against a real database
Acceptance Criteria: docker compose up -d starts PostgreSQL 16; Database decidr_code created; Connection string works from Node.js

Summary: As a developer, I want proper git configuration so that generated files are excluded
Acceptance Criteria: .gitignore excludes node_modules, dist, data, .cj, .env, src-tauri/binaries, pgdata

Summary: As a developer, I want the sidecar package scaffolded so that the Node server can be compiled to a binary later
Acceptance Criteria: sidecar/ directory with package.json, tsconfig, build script; build.sh compiles with pkg

Summary: As a developer, I want hook scripts scaffolded so that the event system has scripts to execute
Acceptance Criteria: hooks/ directory with SessionStart.sh, TicketCreated.sh, TicketMoved.sh, PostSave.sh

Summary: As a developer, I want TypeScript type definitions so that all packages share a single source of truth for data shapes
Acceptance Criteria: All types exported from @decidr-code/core; Ticket, Reasoning, TreeNode, LogEntry, HookEvent defined; Union string types

Summary: As a developer, I want a PostgreSQL schema with proper relations so that data is stored efficiently
Acceptance Criteria: 7 tables created; Foreign keys with CASCADE; Indexes on status, priority, confidence; JSONB for tree and logs; Clean on fresh DB

Summary: As a developer, I want a database connection module so that all repositories share a single client
Acceptance Criteria: Reads DATABASE_URL from env; Single drizzle instance exported; Handles errors gracefully

Summary: As a developer, I want repository classes so that each table has clean CRUD operations
Acceptance Criteria: Each repo under 60 lines; ticket-repo includes joins; All repos return typed results

Summary: As a developer, I want service classes so that business logic is separated from data access
Acceptance Criteria: Services orchestrate repos + hooks; ticket-service handles CRUD with changelog; reasoning-service validates trees

Summary: As a developer, I want a hook execution system so that bash scripts fire on events
Acceptance Criteria: fireHook() logs to DB AND executes bash script; 5s timeout; Failures logged but non-blocking

Summary: As a developer, I want shared constants so that columns, priorities, tags, and node types are defined once
Acceptance Criteria: Constants exported from core; Used by all packages

Summary: As a frontend developer, I want a REST API so that the React app can read and write tickets
Acceptance Criteria: JSON responses with CORS; Ticket CRUD endpoints; Comments endpoints; Error responses with status codes; PORT from env

Summary: As a frontend developer, I want reasoning and stats endpoints so that the UI can display decision trees and dashboards
Acceptance Criteria: Reasoning GET/PUT by ticket ID; Stats aggregation endpoint; Hooks log endpoint

Summary: As a developer, I want a Claude API proxy endpoint so that the frontend can trigger ticket processing without exposing the API key
Acceptance Criteria: POST /api/process accepts ticketId; Reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from env; Saves reasoning to DB; Handles errors

Summary: As a developer, I want HTTP utility functions so that route handlers are clean
Acceptance Criteria: parseBody, sendJSON, sendError, CORS middleware

Summary: As a Cursor/Claude Code user, I want MCP tools so that I can create and manage tickets from inside my editor
Acceptance Criteria: 7 tools registered via stdio; Proper zod schemas; Tools call core services; Cursor lists tools correctly

Summary: As an editor, I want MCP resources so that I can read current board state and ticket details
Acceptance Criteria: 3 resources with proper URIs; Board grouped by column; Ticket by ID; Stats aggregation

Summary: As an editor, I want MCP prompts so that I can analyze tickets and review diffs with pre-built system prompts
Acceptance Criteria: 2 prompts registered; analyze-ticket returns system+user pair; review-diff for code review

Summary: As a developer, I want editor config files so that MCP connects automatically
Acceptance Criteria: .cursor/mcp.json; .claude/settings.json; .cursorrules; CLAUDE.md

Summary: As a developer using any editor, I want to create tickets by dropping JSON files so that I don't need MCP support
Acceptance Criteria: JSON in .decidr/inbox/ creates ticket; Inbox file deleted after processing; Invalid JSON goes to .decidr/errors/

Summary: As a developer, I want the current board state written to a file so that any tool can read it
Acceptance Criteria: .decidr/board.json always current; Updated after mutations; .decidr/outbox/{id}.json after reasoning

Summary: As a user, I want a React app shell with navigation so that I can switch between Board, Hooks, and Stats views
Acceptance Criteria: Header with logo and progress; Nav tabs switch views; Toolbar with search, filters, new button; Dark theme

Summary: As a user, I want layout components so that the app has a consistent header, navigation, and toolbar
Acceptance Criteria: Header.tsx, NavTabs.tsx, Toolbar.tsx all under 35 lines

Summary: As a user, I want a kanban board so that I can see all tickets organized by status column
Acceptance Criteria: 5 columns; Ticket counts; Cards with ID, title, priority, tags, indicators; Search + filter; Click opens detail

Summary: As a user, I want shared UI components so that badges, tags, and avatars are consistent
Acceptance Criteria: Modal, Badge, Tag, Avatar, ProgressBar, EmptyState — all reusable

Summary: As a user, I want a ticket detail modal so that I can view all information about a ticket in one place
Acceptance Criteria: Modal on card click; Header with badges + move buttons; 5 tabs; ESC to close

Summary: As a user, I want a side-by-side diff viewer so that I can see what code Claude changed
Acceptance Criteria: Two panes: BEFORE red / AFTER green; File path header; Line numbers; Empty state

Summary: As a user, I want to trigger Claude processing from the UI so that reasoning and diffs are generated
Acceptance Criteria: Process button calls /api/process; Spinner during processing; Reasoning tab populated on completion

Summary: As a user, I want an interactive decision tree so that I can understand WHY Claude made each choice
Acceptance Criteria: Recursive tree with 8 node types; Expand/collapse; Detail text; Legend; Summary bar; Toggle tree/logs view

Summary: As a user, I want a comment thread so that I can discuss tickets with Claude
Acceptance Criteria: Avatar-based comments; Author + timestamp; Input with Enter-to-send; Optimistic update

Summary: As a user, I want a history timeline so that I can see every action taken on a ticket
Acceptance Criteria: Vertical timeline; Colored dots (purple=Claude, amber=user); Action text + metadata

Summary: As a user, I want a media drop zone so that I can attach screenshots and GIFs to tickets
Acceptance Criteria: Dashed drop zone; Drag visual feedback; File type indicators

Summary: As a user, I want a ticket creation modal so that I can add new tickets with all details
Acceptance Criteria: Title required; Description, priority picker, tag picker, code attachment; Create disabled until title filled; Instant board update

Summary: As a user, I want a hooks log panel so that I can see what events have fired
Acceptance Criteria: Reverse chronological; Color-coded event badges; Payload summary; Auto-refresh 5s

Summary: As a user, I want a stats dashboard so that I can see project health at a glance
Acceptance Criteria: Cards: total, completed, in progress, with reasoning, avg confidence, reasoning time; Color-coded

Summary: As a user, I want Decidr Code to run as a native desktop application so that I don't need a browser
Acceptance Criteria: Native window 1280x800; Sidecar starts on launch; Waits for ready; Kills on close

Summary: As a user, I want a system tray icon so that I can minimize Decidr Code to the background
Acceptance Criteria: Tray icon; Menu: Show Window, Quit; Minimize to tray on close

Summary: As a developer, I want a sidecar build pipeline so that the Node server compiles to a standalone binary
Acceptance Criteria: build.sh produces binary; Correct target triple suffix; Binary runs server + migrations

Summary: As a Cursor/VS Code user, I want a sidebar panel so that I can see the board without leaving my editor
Acceptance Criteria: Activity bar icon; Compact ticket list by status; Click opens detail; Auto-refresh

Summary: As a Cursor/VS Code user, I want commands so that I can create tickets and trigger processing from the command palette
Acceptance Criteria: Create Ticket, List Tickets, Process Ticket commands

Summary: As a developer, I want an API client utility so that the extension can talk to the REST server
Acceptance Criteria: Fetch wrapper; .decidr/ file fallback if server unreachable

Summary: As a developer, I want interactive documentation so that I can understand the architecture, API, and schemas
Acceptance Criteria: Vite app with sidebar nav; CodeBlock + copy; API tester; Schema viewer; Tree demo

Summary: As a developer, I want doc content pages covering every feature
Acceptance Criteria: Pages: Architecture, API Reference, MCP Tools, Schemas, Hooks, Agents, Reasoning, Editor Setup, Extending

Summary: As a developer, I want everything-claude-code integrated so that I get 13+ agents, 50+ skills, and hooks
Acceptance Criteria: ECC cloned as submodule; Agents, skills, rules copied to ~/.claude/; AgentShield scan passes

Summary: As a developer, I want agent definitions optimized for Decidr Code so that tickets route to the right specialist
Acceptance Criteria: 4 agents: bug-triager, refactor-planner, code-reviewer, perf-analyzer

Git Integration & Branch Strategy
Description: Branch naming convention, auto-linking, commit tracking, git hooks, merge detection

Summary: Document Generation
Description: Export project as PDF/Markdown/HTML/DOCX with reasoning trees, diffs, git history

Summary: Visual Flow Diagrams
Description: Architecture flow, data flow, ticket lifecycle, reasoning pipeline visualizations

Summary: As a developer, I want branches auto-linked to tickets by naming convention so that git history is visible on tickets
Acceptance Criteria: Branches matching DC-XXX/* auto-link to ticket; Branch status shown in ticket Git tab; Branch card shows name, status, ahead/behind

Summary: As a developer, I want to see commit history on a ticket's Git tab so that I can track what changed
Acceptance Criteria: Commit list with hash, message, author, date, files; Expandable inline diffs per commit; Files added/modified/deleted shown

Summary: As a developer, I want git hooks that report commits and merges to Decidr Code so that tracking is real-time
Acceptance Criteria: post-commit hook POSTs commit data to API; post-checkout detects new DC-* branches; post-merge reports merge events; install-git-hooks.sh symlinks into .git/hooks/

Summary: As a developer, I want tickets to auto-move when branches are created or merged so that the board stays current
Acceptance Criteria: New DC-* branch moves ticket to in_progress; Merge to main moves ticket to done; Configurable via .decidr/config.json

Summary: As a developer, I want story-level sub-branches so that work within a ticket is organized
Acceptance Criteria: DC-XXX/US-YYY/* branches link to ticket + tagged with story; Sub-branches tracked independently; Merge sub to ticket branch logged

Summary: As a developer, I want branch status indicators on ticket cards so that I can see git state at a glance
Acceptance Criteria: Card shows branch icon + count; Stale branch warning (>7 days); Merged indicator

Summary: As a developer, I want MCP tools for branch creation and git history so that editors can manage git workflow
Acceptance Criteria: create-branch tool; create-story-branch tool; get-git-history tool; link-branch tool

Summary: As a developer, I want a git hook installer so that setting up tracking is one command
Acceptance Criteria: install-hooks command/MCP tool; Symlinks post-commit, post-checkout, post-merge; Works on macOS, Linux, Windows (Git Bash)

Summary: As a developer, I want relevance detection for unlinked commits so that nothing falls through cracks
Acceptance Criteria: Commit message containing DC-XXX links to ticket; File overlap detection (70% confidence); Suggested links shown as 'possibly related' in UI

Summary: As a project lead, I want to export the entire project as a document so that I have a complete record
Acceptance Criteria: Export includes: summary, board overview, per-ticket details (reasoning, diffs, comments, git history), statistics, appendix; Download as file

Summary: As a developer, I want to choose export format (PDF, Markdown, HTML, DOCX) so that I can share in the right format
Acceptance Criteria: 4 format options in export modal; PDF via puppeteer; DOCX via docx library; MD and HTML built-in

Summary: As a developer, I want the export to include reasoning trees, diffs, comments, and git history so that the document is comprehensive
Acceptance Criteria: Reasoning trees rendered as indented text; Diffs with syntax highlighting; Comment threads with author attribution; Git commit log per ticket

Summary: As a developer, I want an export MCP tool so that I can generate documents from my editor
Acceptance Criteria: export-project tool; Input: format, scope (all/filtered/single ticket); Returns file path or content

Summary: As a developer, I want an architecture flow diagram so that I can see how packages connect
Acceptance Criteria: Interactive node graph showing: core, server, mcp, file-bridge, web, vscode-ext; Edges show dependency direction; Click node shows package details

Summary: As a developer, I want a ticket lifecycle visualization so that I can see the state machine with hooks
Acceptance Criteria: 5 states connected by arrows; Hook annotations on each transition; Current ticket highlighted when viewing from ticket detail

Summary: As a developer, I want a data flow diagram so that I can trace requests from UI to database
Acceptance Criteria: Flow: User → React → Zustand → API Client → REST Server → Service → Repository → PostgreSQL; Animated data flow on hover

Summary: As a developer, I want a reasoning pipeline visualization so that I can see how tickets get processed
Acceptance Criteria: Flow: Ticket → Agent Router → Claude API → Parse Response → Save Reasoning → Update Diff → Notify

Summary: As a developer, I want a mini flow in each ticket detail showing its journey so far
Acceptance Criteria: Small horizontal flow in ticket header; Highlights current state; Shows which hooks have fired; Which agent processed it
